input: Reframe 'how do we reduce hospital readmissions?' by questioning what a good discharge outcome really is.

questions.question: If readmission wasn't measured by 30 days, what key time horizon defines 'success'?
questions.why_this_unlocks: Shifting focus from acute failure to sustained recovery timelines

questions.question: What are the non-clinical determinants (housing, food security) that predict stability post-discharge?
questions.why_this_unlocks: Identifying social infrastructure gaps as primary failure points

questions.question: Whose definition of 'good outcome' is prioritized: payer, provider, or patient?
questions.why_this_unlocks: Revealing inherent conflicts of interest in current discharge planning

questions.question: What alternative to 'readmission' measures true functional reintegration into community life?
questions.why_this_unlocks: Creating metrics beyond simple recurrence rates

branch_seeds: Mapping social determinants of health (SDOH) onto discharge protocols
Designing payment models tied to 90-day functional recovery
Developing remote monitoring protocols for high-risk comorbidities (e.g., CHF)
Creating standardized, patient-led discharge education modules

research: Post-acute care payment models (e.g., bundled payments)

candidates: Algorithm factoring SDOH + Comorbidity load for discharge risk scoring
Geospatial platform linking discharge address to verified local social supports (food banks, physical therapy availability)
Patient-reported outcome measures (PROM) focused on daily activity capacity (e.g., stairs climbed, distance walked)
Payment structure rewarding payer/provider for patient self-reported adherence and community engagement metrics over 180 days
Mandatory 'Transition Coordinator' role, paid by outcome achievement, managing home/community logistics post-discharge

pruned.reason: Too focused on payment structure, general concept

combinations.novelty_note: Quantifying predicted stability using functional capacity instead of just clinical markers
Actionable deployment: Risk score dictates intensity/type of human oversight
Tying required physical recovery milestones directly to local resource deployment

dead_ends: Redefining 'discharge' as simply leaving the building

scores.candidate: Post-acute care payment models (e.g., bundled payments)
scores.notes: Industry discussion point; lacks structural novelty.

scores.candidate: Algorithm factoring SDOH + Comorbidity load for discharge risk scoring
scores.notes: Solid refinement, but combination is stronger.

scores.candidate: Geospatial platform linking discharge address to verified local social supports (food banks, physical therapy availability)
scores.notes: Good specificity, but 'Resource Mapping' is a known area.

scores.candidate: Patient-reported outcome measures (PROM) focused on daily activity capacity (e.g., stairs climbed, distance walked)
scores.notes: Specific focus, but PROM is broad.

scores.candidate: Payment structure rewarding payer/provider for patient self-reported adherence and community engagement metrics over 180 days
scores.notes: Too much policy framing; vague mechanism.

unexplored_directions: Integrating cognitive/mental load assessment into discharge planning
Designing 'reverse discharge' systems (returning patients to the hospital if home metrics fail)
feedback_for_curiosity: Test B3/B2 combination with quantifiable negative outcomes (e.g., 'Resource Gap Score').
Force the payment model (B4) to explicitly fund the 'Transition Coordinator' role (B5).

validation_errors: loop[0].critic PASS without candidate meeting threshold
